name: Grundarfjörður
summary: Grundarfjörður is a tiny fishing village on Iceland's western coast, tucked beside a deep fjord and the famous pointy mountain Kirkjufell. Just behind it stands Snæfellsjökull, a volcano with a glacier on top.
funFacts: Kirkjufell means 'Church Mountain' and is the most photographed mountain in Iceland, with a pretty waterfall called Kirkjufellsfoss right in front of it.
Snæfellsjökull is a volcano with a thick cap of ice on top, and a writer named Jules Verne picked it as the secret entrance to the centre of the Earth.
Pods of killer whales swim into the fjord every winter chasing herring, and the village even has a stone sculpture shaped like an orca to celebrate them.
The whole peninsula is nicknamed 'Iceland in miniature' because it has glaciers, lava fields, black beaches and waterfalls all squeezed into one small area.
Only about 900 people live in Grundarfjörður, and the rainbow-painted village church has tall pointy windows that match the shape of Kirkjufell behind it.
thingsToDo: Take a photo of pointy Kirkjufell mountain with Kirkjufellsfoss waterfall splashing in front of it.
Hop on a whale-watching boat from the harbour to look for killer whales and humpback whales.
Drive into Snæfellsjökull National Park to see the glacier-topped volcano up close.
Walk along Djúpalónssandur, a beach covered in smooth, shiny black pebbles instead of sand.
Crawl through Vatnshellir, an 8,000-year-old lava cave that twists deep underground.
Wander Grundarfjörður's little harbour and spot the colourful village church above the houses.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Chubby black-and-white seabird with a bright orange beak that nests in clifftop burrows.

wildlife.name: Arctic Fox
wildlife.description: Small fluffy fox with a thick winter coat that turns snowy white when it snows.

wildlife.name: Icelandic Horse
wildlife.description: Friendly short-legged horse with a shaggy mane and an extra bouncy way of running called the tölt.

wildlife.name: Killer Whale
wildlife.description: Big black-and-white ocean hunter that visits the fjord in winter to chase shoals of herring.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in Icelandic — the language spoken in Grundarfjörður.

images.alt: The pointy Kirkjufell mountain rises behind the small Kirkjufellsfoss waterfall in Iceland.
A view from above of the small seaside village of Grundarfjörður with Kirkjufell mountain nearby.
The bright village church of Grundarfjörður with its tall pointy windows.
Snæfellsjökull, a snow-covered volcano with a glacier on top, beneath a blue Iceland sky.
Smooth shiny black pebbles cover a beach where dark cliffs meet the ocean at Djúpalónssandur.
Inside Vatnshellir lava cave, with rocky walls lit by torches.
An Atlantic puffin with a bright orange beak holding a beakful of small silver fish.
An Arctic fox in its snowy white winter coat sits on the snow in Iceland.
A shaggy Icelandic horse stands in a green field with mountains behind it.
A black-and-white killer whale leaps high out of the sea.
